Notes: Most likely initial access vector through email or social engineering
Less frequent than phishing but severe if database availability is lost
Possible if segmentation or ACLs are too permissive

Threat Scenario: Alert Fatigue Leading to Missed Advanced Threat Activity
Notes: High alert volume may cause delayed detection of multi-stage attacks

Threat Scenario: Data Loss through Application Persistence Bug
Affected Asset(s): Client Application; Local Storage; Backend Records
Notes: Known baseline weakness that may cause record disappearance or corruption
Compromise of privileged accounts would significantly expand attacker access

Threat Scenario: Malformed API Input Causing Data Integrity Loss
Affected Asset(s): Core API Gateway; Customer Records Database
Notes: Weak validation could allow tampered or malformed financial records
Improper routing or ACL enforcement may allow attacker pivoting
